title: Andy CLI headless run config v1
description: Schema for the config file consumed by `andy-cli run --headless --config <path>`. See Epic AQ (rivoli-ai/andy-cli#44) and docs/headless-runtime.md for the full semantics.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bump to 2 introduces a new schema file (v2.json) and a separate compatibility layer; do not overload v1.

run_id.description: Correlates stdout events and artifacts with the andy-containers Run entity (rivoli-ai/andy-containers#103).

slug.description: Agent identifier from andy-agents (Epic W W5 seed). Lowercase ASCII, dash-separated, 2-64 chars.

revision.description: Optional pin to a specific agent revision. Null/absent = head.

instructions.description: Resolved system prompt: agent.instructions concatenated with delegation_contract.objective.

output_format.description: Optional hint for the agent's final output: a free-form label like 'plain' or a schema reference like 'json-triage-output-v1'.

provider.description: LLM provider. Pluggable via Andy.Llm adapter registry.

id.description: Model identifier as recognised by the provider (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6', 'gpt-5-medium').

api_key_ref.description: How to resolve the API key. Supported prefixes: 'env:NAME' (read env var), 'secret-store:NAME' (future). Never the bare key value.

tools.description: Concrete tool bindings this run is allowed to call, resolved from the agent's skills × allowed_tools.

name.description: Tool identifier as seen by the agent.

command.description: Default argv the agent prepends. The agent appends its own args.

root.description: Absolute path inside the container to the mounted workspace (typically /workspace).

branch.description: Git branch the run operates on.

env_vars.description: Additional env vars injected into the agent process. Reserved: ANDY_PROXY_URL, ANDY_TOKEN, ANDY_MCP_URL are always set by the container runtime (Epic Y5).

file.description: Path to write the final structured output. Atomic write (tmp + rename). Absent on failure before output stage.

stream.description: Where the structured event stream goes. 'stdout' is the default; 'fifo' names a separate path (set via event_sink.path).

nats_subject.description: NATS subject the container runtime (Epic AP6) will fan events to. Agent does not publish directly.

path.description: Absolute path to a named FIFO when output.stream == 'fifo'.

policy_id.description: Resolved policy UUID from andy-rbac (Epic V). Null-ish until V lands platform-wide.

boundaries.description: Policy-derived boundary tags (e.g. 'read-only', 'no-prod', 'draft-only'). Runtime uses these as guard-rails; the authoritative evaluation happens server-side at each tool call.

max_iterations.description: Hard cap on agent loop iterations. Exceeded = exit code 4 (timeout).
